task_completion.description: Set the punch range from 8.0 to 14.0 seconds and enable punch-in and punch-out.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The punch range indicators (usually red markers/lines in the timeline ruler) are set at the 8.0s and 14.0s marks. The 'Punch In' and 'Punch Out' buttons in the transport control area are active/highlighted.

task_completion.description: Set the loop range from 6.0 to 16.0 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The loop range indicators (usually green markers/lines in the timeline ruler) are set at the 6.0s and 16.0s marks.

task_completion.description: Create a named marker at the 8.0s punch-in boundary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A location marker is visible on the timeline exactly at the 8.0s mark, with a descriptive name such as 'Punch In'.

task_completion.description: Create a named marker at the 14.0s punch-out boundary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A location marker is visible on the timeline exactly at the 14.0s mark, with a descriptive name such as 'Punch Out'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the audio track is record-armed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The record-enable button (typically a small circle) on the specific audio track's header is clicked and highlighted (usually red).
The 'File' menu is opened and 'Save' is clicked, or the session window title bar no longer shows an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used the application's GUI to configure the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking and dragging within the digital audio workstation (DAW) interface, rather than using a terminal to edit session XML files directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual audio software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The DAW interface (Ardour) is clearly visible in the screenshots, not a text editor or a mock application.

integrity.description: The agent manually set the markers and ranges.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of step-by-step GUI interaction (mouse movements, context menus, transport bar clicks) rather than a sudden jump to a fully configured state via an external script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The session sample rate is 44100 Hz. The VLM should verify the timecodes either in seconds or in samples: Punch In is at 8.0s (352800 samples), Punch Out is at 14.0s (617400 samples), Loop Start is at 6.0s (264600 samples), and Loop End is at 16.0s (705600 samples). Look for these exact values in the timeline ruler, marker list, or transport/range configuration dialogs.
